items.title: Representing ML datasets
items.description: In supervised learning, examples are commonly stored as a matrix X where rows are samples and columns are features. Matrix operations such as centering, scaling and feature selection map cleanly to operations on X.

items.title: Weight matrices in neural networks
items.description: Fully connected layers use matrices to store learnable weights. Even without diving into full matrix multiplication, understanding shapes and basic operations is essential for reasoning about how activations move between layers.

items.title: Computer graphics transformations
items.description: 2D and 3D transformations such as rotations, scalings and translations are encoded as matrices. Applying a transformation to all points in a scene corresponds to multiplying a matrix of points by a transformation matrix.

items.title: Solving linear systems in optimization
items.description: Many optimization problems reduce to solving or approximately solving linear systems A x = b. The matrix A captures constraints or curvature information, and basic matrix operations underlie iterative solvers and factorization methods.

items.title: Covariance and correlation matrices
items.description: In statistics and ML, covariance and correlation matrices summarize pairwise relationships between features. Understanding matrix structure and operations helps interpret such matrices and manipulate them in pipelines.
